 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

/* RISC-V semihosting write-string. Safe in QEMU1 (phase 1) and QEMU2
 * (phase 3); both QEMUs are spawned with -semihosting-config
 * enable=on,target=native so the magic slli/ebreak/srai triple is
 * intercepted before reaching mtvec. MUST NOT be called inside the
 * vsim span (between rt_phase_qemu1_drain and rt_phase_vsim_drain):
 * vsim does not implement semihosting, the ebreak routes to
 * m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loops. */
static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
{
  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
                       ".option norvc\n"
                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
                       "ebreak\n"
                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
                       ".option pop\n"
                       :
                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
                       : "memory");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf

toolUseResult.newString:
# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via rt_semihost_writes(), matching the QEMU2 launch in
# qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from doing any I/O --
# sim_control MMIO and semihosting are both off-limits there.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

content.content:
145	__attribute__((used, noreturn)) void _exit(int exit_status)
146	{
147	  /* In the hybrid round-trip flow, _exit is only reached in QEMU 2 (vsim
148	     itself terminates at the csrwi 0x7C0, 1 drain marker, well before main
149	     returns). QEMU 2 has -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native, so
150	     SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED reaches the host with the right status. The ebreak
151	     triple matches hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc.
152	
153	     We deliberately do NOT also poke EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR (0xE0080000) here:

__attribute__((used, noreturn)) void _exit(int exit_status)
{
  /* In the hybrid round-trip flow, _exit is only reached in QEMU 2 (vsim
     itself terminates at the csrwi 0x7C0, 1 drain marker, well before main

     mdcause (CSR 0x7C9) on default -cpu rv64 and re-traps forever.

     directly and short-circuits the round-trip. */

- `rt_c_matmul.c`, `rt_c_v_matmul.c`: dropped phase-2 status printfs; kernel runs silent.

message.content: if I don't wanna link special libgloss.c for vsim, is it ok? how to satisfy this request?

content.text:
Yes - now that phase 2 is I/O-free, libgloss is only invoked in QEMU phases where semihosting works. Three options:

**Option A (minimal diff): rewrite `runtime/libgloss.c` to be semihosting-only.**

Drop all `frontend_syscall` paths and the `_write` sim_control poke. Keep `_sbrk` (no I/O) and the existing semihosting `_exit`. Replace `_write` with a SYS_WRITE/SYS_WRITE0 semihosting call. The file becomes portable: same library works under QEMU virt, QEMU2 of the hybrid flow, or anywhere with `-semihosting-config enable=on`. The "special for vsim" label disappears - it's just "RISC-V semihosting libgloss".

Required syscalls drop from ~20 down to: `_exit`, `_sbrk`, `_write` (and possibly `_close`/`_fstat`/`_lseek`/`_read` as stubs that errno out, because newlib's stdio bootstrap may reference them at link time).

**Option B (cleaner, larger diff): drop libgloss + newlib entirely.**

`rt_c_printf.c` is the only fixture pulling newlib (for `vsnprintf`). Replace it with an inline tiny formatter or rewrite as `rt_semihost_writes`-of-literals. Then every fixture compiles `-nostdlib` against just crt0 + trap + handler + a 5-line semihosting-_exit stub. No `_sbrk`, no `_write`, no newlib.

**Option C: leave libgloss as-is.**

After the phase-2 rule, libgloss's HTIF paths are dead code - never reached. Has zero runtime cost, but stale code lingers.

**My recommendation: A.** It keeps `vsnprintf` available (so rt_c_printf still exercises newlib's vfprintf machinery), removes the vsim-specific MMIO, and is a ~60-line file delete + ~30-line rewrite. Want me to do A?

message.content:
You didn't understand my question well. 
Rethink from a higher level, I only have an executable but needs to run on QEMU and VSIM (HYbrid mode)
So, I can only choose semihosting for QEMU or special libgloss.c for VSIM, right? 
My question is if I decide to support semihosting for QEMU, what kind of new rules of code snippet I need to set at phase 2 (vsim) so that I don't need to link the special libgloss.c

content.text:
Right. Two IO backends: semihosting (QEMU-only, traps on vsim) or vsim-HTIF libgloss (vsim-only, faults on QEMU). Pick one, the other half of the run must not invoke it.

If you pick **semihosting** and want to drop the vsim-libgloss, the phase-2 rule must be stricter than what we just set. Today's rule only forbids the vsim HTIF MMIO; it must also forbid semihosting and anything that transitively reaches `_write`/`_read`/etc., because those would now route to semihosting and trap on vsim.

**Phase-2 rule (semihosting-binary edition):**

Forbidden inside the span:
1. RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`rt_semihost_writes`, any inline `slli/ebreak/srai` block) - vsim's mtvec would catch it.
2. Newlib stdio / FILE / fd calls: `printf`, `puts`, `fprintf`, `fwrite`, `fopen`, `fread`, `fseek`, `fclose`, `unlink`, `getc`, `perror`, `assert` (-> `__assert_func`), `abort` (writes to stderr).
3. `errno`-touching syscalls that go through libgloss stubs (`time`, `gettimeofday`, `stat`, `open`, `read`, `write`, `close`, `lseek`).
4. Stream flush at the boundary: `setvbuf(_IONBF, ...)` then `printf` straddling `rt_phase_vsim_drain()` - the trailing flush would land in vsim.

Allowed inside the span (pure CPU / memory, no syscall):
- `vsnprintf` / `vsprintf` into a caller-supplied buffer
- `memcpy`, `memset`, `memmove`, `memcmp`, `strlen`, `strcmp`, `strcpy`
- `<math.h>` (purely functional)
- Inline asm on GPRs / FPRs / V regs, loads/stores to mapped RAM
- `setjmp` / `longjmp` (pure register save/restore)

Mechanical consequence: build the strings inside phase 2 with `vsnprintf` into a `char buf[N]`, then call `rt_semihost_writes(buf)` *after* `rt_phase_vsim_drain()` in phase 3 (or before `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()` in phase 1).

Then `libgloss.c` collapses to the semihosting variant: `_exit` (already semihosting), `_sbrk` (no IO), and `_write -> SYS_WRITE/SYS_WRITE0` plus EIO-returning stubs for `_read`/`_close`/`_lseek`/`_fstat` so the linker resolves newlib's references. No HTIF, no sim_control, no `frontend_syscall`. Same binary, same library, runs everywhere semihosting is enabled.

For enforcement: a CI grep that, per fixture, awk-extracts the bytes between `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()` and `rt_phase_vsim_drain()` and fails if any forbidden symbol (`printf|fopen|rt_semihost_writes|...`) appears. Want me to add that + rewrite `libgloss.c` to the semihosting-only form?

lastPrompt: show me your understanding

content.text:
**The constraint**

One ELF, runs on two simulators that share the same registers/memory but trap differently. The binary can carry at most one IO backend:

```
                       phase 1    phase 2    phase 3
                       (QEMU)     (vsim)     (QEMU)
backend A: semihost     works      TRAPS      works    <- you want this
backend B: vsim-HTIF    faults     works      faults   <- current code
```

Backend A = magic `slli/ebreak/srai` triple, intercepted by QEMU. Vsim doesn't intercept it -> falls through to `mtvec`.
Backend B = MMIO writes to `0xE0080004` (sim_control). QEMU virt has no device there -> store-access-fault.

You can't have both. So the IO mechanism that's *baked into the binary* must be exactly one of the two, and the phase running on the wrong simulator must not touch it.

**You're picking A (semihosting). The rule on phase 2 follows from this:**

Phase 2 must call no function whose code path ever reaches the magic triple. The triple is hidden inside libgloss `_write`, `_exit`, and inside `rt_semihost_writes`. Anything that transitively reaches them is forbidden.

```
       phase 2 code path
              |
              v
   any function call?
              |
       +------+------+
       |             |
   pure CPU      libgloss / rt_semihost_writes
   (vsnprintf,        |
    memcpy,           v
    math,         ebreak triple
    inline asm)       |
       |              v
     SAFE          TRAPS in vsim
```

So the prohibition isn't just "no printf" - it's "no transitive path to the ebreak triple." Practically that means:

- No `rt_semihost_writes` (direct triple)
- No newlib stdio (`printf`, `puts`, `fprintf`, `fopen`, `fread`, `fwrite`, ...) - they bottom out in `_write`
- No `assert` / `abort` / `perror` - they reach `_write` via stderr
- No `_exit` / `exit` - they're a triple too
- No `time` / `stat` / any syscall stub - they're triples

What's allowed: anything that touches only registers and memory. `vsnprintf` into a buffer is fine; the buffer can be emitted *after* `rt_phase_vsim_drain()`. `memcpy`, `memcmp`, math, inline asm including V instructions: all fine.

**Consequence for libgloss.c**

Once phase 2 is silent in that strict sense, `libgloss.c` no longer needs the vsim-special MMIO paths. It collapses to a pure-semihosting file: `_write` via SYS_WRITE, `_exit` via SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (already there), `_sbrk` as a bump pointer (no IO), plus EIO-returning stubs for `_read`/`_close`/`_lseek`/`_fstat` so newlib still links. That single libgloss is no longer "special for vsim" - it's the standard RISC-V semihosting libgloss, works on QEMU, works as dead-code-only on vsim because phase 2 never calls into it.

**What the binary does in each phase under this model**

| Phase | Runs on | Allowed IO | Forbidden |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | QEMU | semihosting (printf, rt_semihost_writes, ...) | sim_control MMIO |
| 2 | vsim | nothing | semihosting + sim_control + any newlib syscall stub |
| 3 | QEMU | semihosting (printf, rt_semihost_writes, ...) | sim_control MMIO |

Does this match your model?

Bad wording on my part. `errno` itself is just a thread-local int - reading or writing it is pure memory, totally fine in phase 2.

What I meant to flag was the **syscall stubs themselves** (`_open`, `_read`, `_write`, `_close`, `_lseek`, `_fstat`, `_stat`, `_gettimeofday`, `_unlink`, ...). In the semihosting-libgloss world, every one of those wraps a magic `slli/ebreak/srai` triple. The "errno-touching" phrase was just a sloppy way of pointing at "the libgloss syscall layer" - errno is a side effect of those calls, not the reason they're forbidden.

The forbidden-ness flows entirely from rule 1 (no path to the magic triple). The clean restatement:

- Forbidden: any libc/libgloss function that issues a syscall (i.e., reaches the magic triple)
- Allowed: anything that only touches registers + memory, including reading/writing `errno`

Option A = rewrite `runtime/libgloss.c` to be a **semihosting-only** libgloss. Concretely:

- `_write(fd, ptr, len)`: replace the `*(0xE0080004) = byte` loop with a `SYS_WRITE` (0x05) or per-byte `SYS_WRITEC` (0x03) semihosting call.
- `_exit(status)`: already semihosting (`SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Keep as-is.
- `_sbrk`: bump pointer. No IO. Keep as-is.
- `_read`, `_close`, `_lseek`, `_fstat`: collapse each to a one-liner that sets `errno = ENOSYS` and returns -1 (or use semihosting equivalents if you want fopen/fread to actually work in QEMU). Newlib references them at link time, so they must exist as symbols, but at runtime nothing in the fixtures calls them.
- Delete: `_open`, `_openat`, `_access`, `_faccessat`, `_link`, `_unlink`, `_rename`, `_stat`, `_lstat`, `_fstatat`, `_getcwd`, `_gettimeofday`, `_flen`, the `frontend_syscall` plumbing, the `htif.h` include, all the `SYSCALL_N` macros, and the `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` / `EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR` constants.

Result: one ~30-line file, no HTIF, no `0xE008_xxxx` MMIO, no `frontend_syscall`. The "special-for-vsim" label is gone - it's the standard RISC-V semihosting libgloss, identical to what a bare-metal newlib + `-semihosting-config` setup uses anywhere else. Phase 2 still must not call any of these (per the rule we just discussed), but the file itself is no longer vsim-aware.

message.content: Actually, I don't understand why we need to rewrite runtime/libgloss.c?

content.text:
You're right - you don't need to. I oversold it earlier.

Once phase 2 is I/O-free and phases 1/3 use `rt_semihost_writes` (which bypasses libgloss and goes straight to the semihosting triple), libgloss is reached at runtime only via:

- `_exit` - already semihosting (`SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`), works on QEMU2.
- `_sbrk` - bump pointer, no IO.

The HTIF MMIO bodies inside `_write`/`_open`/`_read`/... are now **dead code at runtime**. They're linked but never called, because nothing in any fixture invokes newlib stdio anymore. The binary functions correctly on both QEMU and vsim with the file untouched.

A rewrite is only justified if you want `printf` (and friends) to **work** in phases 1 and 3 - that requires `_write` to use semihosting instead of `*0xE0080004 = byte`. None of the current fixtures need that; they all emit via `rt_semihost_writes` directly.

So: keep `libgloss.c` as-is. It's vsim-aware in source, vsim-irrelevant at runtime.

lastPrompt: Actually, I don't understand why we need to rewrite runtime/libgloss.c?

message.content:
why do we need to call special rt_semihost_writes for printing? 
we could call standard printf and link -mvh, QEMU 1 and QEMU 2 could handle it.

content: write a hello world to test it
Now, no any system calls are allowed in vsim span, only QEMU 1 & 2

content.text:
You're right. That's cleaner.

Reason the project has a custom `runtime/libgloss.c`: the codebase was originally vsim-only, where the only way to emit a byte was the HTIF MMIO at `0xE0080004`. The whole `frontend_syscall` + `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` apparatus was written to make `newlib` work against that target. `rt_semihost_writes` got added later as a single-purpose escape hatch for QEMU2 output, because `printf` (going through the vsim-libgloss's `_write`) would fault on QEMU. The asymmetry is a residue of the project's history, not a design.

With the phase-2-is-silent rule in place, the right thing is:

1. Drop `runtime/libgloss.c` and `htif.h`, drop `runtime/libgloss.c` from the link line.
2. Link the toolchain's semihosting libgloss instead (the Andes nds-newlib has a semihost variant - whether it's `--specs=semihost.specs`, `-lsemihost`, or `-mvh`, that's the right knob; pick whichever your toolchain's docs name).
3. Delete `rt_semihost_writes` from `rt_c_helpers.h`.
4. Rewrite fixtures' QEMU1/QEMU2 output to just call `printf` (or `fputs`, etc.).

After that the picture is symmetric: standard `newlib` + standard semihosting libgloss, same as any bare-metal RISC-V QEMU target. The hybrid simulator brings nothing custom into the binary. The only project-specific code in the fixture sources is the two `csrwi 0x7C0` markers, which are trigger signals to the plugin / vsim - not IO.

Phase-2 rule unchanged: don't call `printf` (or anything else that reaches the magic triple) between `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()` and `rt_phase_vsim_drain()`. The reason is the same as before; only the IO backend differs.

Want me to do the swap? I'd need to confirm the exact link flag your `nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d` toolchain uses for the semihosting libgloss before touching the Makefile.

message.content: write a hello world to test it
Now, no any system calls are allowed in vsim span, only QEMU 1 & 2

%rename link	semihost_link

*lib:

./mext-zc/libgloss_vh.a
./mext-dsp/libgloss_vh.a
./libgloss_vh.a
./mcmodel-medlow/libgloss_vh.a
./mcmodel-large/libgloss_vh.a
./mtune-andes-45-series/libgloss_vh.a
---
== nano.specs ==
%rename link                nano_link
%rename link_gcc_c_sequence                nano_link_gcc_c_sequence
%rename cpp		nano_cpp

*cpp:
-isystem =/include/newlib-nano %(nano_cpp)

*nano_libc:
-lc_nano

== nosys.specs ==
%rename link_gcc_c_sequence                nosys_link_gcc_c_sequence

*nosys_libgloss:
-lnosys

*nosys_libc:
%{!specs=nano.specs:-lc} %{specs=nano.specs:-lc_nano}

*link_gcc_c_sequence:
%(nosys_link_gcc_c_sequence) --start-group %G %(nosys_libc) %(nosys_libgloss) --end-group
== semihost.specs ==
# Spec file for semihosting syscalls.

%rename lib	semihost_lib
%rename link	semihost_link

*lib:
--start-group -lc -lsemihost --end-group

*link:
%(semihost_link) %:replace-outfile(-lgloss -lsemihost)
== sim.specs ==
# Spec file for gdb simulator.

%rename lib	sim_lib
%rename link	sim_link

*lib:
--start-group -lc -lsim --end-group

*link:
%(sim_link) %:replace-outfile(-lgloss -lsim)

file.content:
#=========================================================================
# crt0.S : Entry point for RISC-V user programs
#=========================================================================

#ifndef CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
#define CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS 0
#endif

#define mcache_ctl 0x7ca
#define mmisc_ctl 0x7d0

  .text
  .global _start
  .type   _start, @function
_start:
  # Enable icache
  csrsi   mcache_ctl, 0x1

#ifdef ENABLE_DCACHE_COHERENCE
  # Enable dcache coherence management
  li      a0, 1 << 19
  csrs    mcache_ctl, a0
1:
  csrr    a0, mcache_ctl
#if __nds_v5
  bbc     a0, 20, 1b
#else
  srli    a1, a0, 20
  andi    a1, a1, 1
  beqz    a1, 1b
#endif
#endif

  # Enable d-cache and hardware prefetch
  ori     a0, a0, 0x703
  csrw    mcache_ctl, a0

  .weak   _stack
  # Initialize stack pointer (if defined)
  lla     a0, _stack
  beqz    a0, 1f
  mv      sp, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  slli    a0, a0, 12
  sub     sp, sp, a0
#endif

  # Initialize global pointer
.option push
.option norelax
1:lla gp, __global_pointer$
.option pop

#ifdef __nds_v5
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg
  bbc     a0, 3, 1f
  la      a1, _ITB_BASE_
  csrrw   x0, uitb, a1
1:
#if __riscv_xlen >= 64
  bbc     a0, 52, 1f
#else
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg2
  bbc     a0, 20, 1f
#endif
  csrr    a0, mrvarch_cfg
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  la      a0, __jvt_base$
  csrw    jvt, a0
#endif
1:

  # Set mstatus.fs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 13)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Set mstatus.vs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 10)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Enable unaligned memory access (mmisc_ctl.UNA)
  li      a0, (1 << 6)
  csrs    mmisc_ctl, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss

  li      a1, 0xa55a
.Lother_hart_wait_loop:
  ld      a0, other_hart_boot_flag
  bne     a0, a1, .Lother_hart_wait_loop
  j       .Lmain
#else
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss
  li      a0, 0x01234571
  li      a1, 0xe0080000
  sw      a0, 0(a1)
1:
  j       1b
#endif

  # Clear the bss segment
.Lclear_bss:
  la      a0, _edata
  la      a2, _end
  sub     a2, a2, a0
  li      a1, 0
  call    memset


#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  li      a0, 0xa55a
  lla     a1, other_hart_boot_flag
  sd      a0, 0(a1)
  fence
#endif

  call init_mtvec

.Lmain:
  li      a0, 0
  li      a1, 0
  li      a2, 0
  call    main

  call    _exit

.Lloop:
  j       .Lloop

  .size   _start, .-_start

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  .data
  .p2align 3
  .global other_hart_boot_flag
  .type   other_hart_boot_flag, @object
other_hart_boot_flag:
  .8byte 0
  .size other_hart_boot_flag, . - other_hart_boot_flag
#endif

RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Virtual-hosting (vh) fixtures: standard newlib + toolchain libgloss_vh
# provides semihosting-based _write/_exit/_sbrk. No custom runtime/libgloss.c

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Virtual-hosting (vh) fixtures: standard newlib + toolchain libgloss_vh
# provides semihosting-based _write/_exit/_sbrk. No custom runtime/libgloss.c
# in the link, so the binary carries zero vsim-specific MMIO paths and runs
# on any RISC-V target where -semihosting-config enable=on is honoured.
RT_C_VH_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello_vh
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# vh fixtures: crt0+trap+handler only (NO libgloss.c). -nodefaultlibs
# suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc; we re-add them
# in --start-group with -lgloss_vh inserted so newlib's syscall stubs
# resolve to the semihosting (virtual-hosting) implementations.
RT_C_VH_CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
RT_C_VH_RUNTIME := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_VH_LIBS    := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)
